metadata.provenance: Original Ruhroh bundled scenario authored around synthetic CSV reconciliation fixtures.
metadata.networkRationale: Allows package installation for a Vite React TypeScript app.
metadata.contaminationNotes: Original workflow and bundled CSV assets; no canonical public solution.

scenarioContext: This is a local data-workflow app-development task with provided CSV assets.
The prompt-assets/csv-reconciliation-people directory is the source material supplied to the run-agent.
The benchmark value is CSV handling, reconciliation workflow, review state, and export/import behavior.
goalRubric: The final app is a Vite React + TypeScript app that can be installed and run locally by a reviewer.
The app uses or embeds the supplied Source A and Source B CSV sample data.
The app normalizes people records, detects likely duplicates, flags missing records, and surfaces field conflicts.
The user can resolve conflicts, choose winning field values, mark duplicates reviewed, and filter by conflict type.
Reconciliation counts update as review decisions change.
Review state persists locally and the cleaned result can be exported as JSON or CSV and imported back.
evidenceGuidance: Inspect the copied prompt assets and final workspace before judging.
Run build/start commands and exercise the reconciliation workflow when practical.
Prefer evidence from app behavior and exported data over source text matches.

calibrationCases.inputSummary: The app displays both CSV files but offers no duplicate detection, conflict resolution, review state, or cleaned export.
calibrationCases.rationale: Viewing data alone does not satisfy the requested reconciliation workflow.
